old_value: {"id":"compress-claude-bn9","title":"CLI examples for all compression formats","description":"Create CLI examples demonstrating all three layers (L1 pure, L2 async, L3 sync) for each compression format: flate, gzip, zlib, lzw, bzip2, and checksum. Examples live in lib/examples/ with moon.pkg configs.","acceptance_criteria":"All examples compile and run. L1/L3 examples work on wasm-gc, L2 async examples work on native target with #cfg guards.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T02:53:11Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T02:53:11Z"}

{"id":"compress-claude-2tv","title":"Optimize CRC-32 with slicing-by-8","description":"CRC-32 is 427x slower than Go because Go uses hardware SSE4.2/CLMUL instructions. Since MoonBit/WASM can't use hardware CRC, implement slicing-by-8 (process 8 bytes per iteration with 8 precomputed tables) to get ~8x improvement over current byte-at-a-time approach.

**Current**: 110.9 µs/10KB (byte-at-a-time table lookup)
**Go**: 0.26 µs/10KB (hardware CRC)
**Target**: ~14 µs/10KB (slicing-by-8, still ~54x slower than hardware but 8x better)","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Implement 8-table slicing-by-8 CRC-32
- [ ] CRC-32 benchmark improves by at least 5x
- [ ] All checksum tests still pass","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T02:32:50Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T02:32:50Z"}

{"id":"compress-claude-2tv","title":"Optimize CRC-32 with slicing-by-8","description":"CRC-32 is 427x slower than Go because Go uses hardware SSE4.2/CLMUL instructions. Since MoonBit/WASM can't use hardware CRC, implement slicing-by-8 (process 8 bytes per iteration with 8 precomputed tables) to get ~8x improvement over current byte-at-a-time approach.

**Current**: 110.9 µs/10KB (byte-at-a-time table lookup)
**Go**: 0.26 µs/10KB (hardware CRC)
**Target**: ~14 µs/10KB (slicing-by-8, still ~54x slower than hardware but 8x better)","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Implement 8-table slicing-by-8 CRC-32
- [ ] CRC-32 benchmark improves by at least 5x
- [ ] All checksum tests still pass","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T02:32:50Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T02:59:16Z"}

{"id":"compress-claude-2jw","title":"Optimize Adler-32 with SWAR/block processing","description":"Adler-32 is 45x slower than Go. Implement block processing (NMAX=5552 chunk optimization) and potentially SWAR tricks to reduce overhead.

**Current**: 97.4 µs/10KB
**Go**: 2.15 µs/10KB","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Adler-32 benchmark improves by at least 3x
- [ ] All checksum tests still pass","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T02:33:00Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T02:33:00Z"}

{"id":"compress-claude-2jw","title":"Optimize Adler-32 with SWAR/block processing","description":"Adler-32 is 45x slower than Go. Implement block processing (NMAX=5552 chunk optimization) and potentially SWAR tricks to reduce overhead.

**Current**: 97.4 µs/10KB
**Go**: 2.15 µs/10KB","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Adler-32 benchmark improves by at least 3x
- [ ] All checksum tests still pass","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T02:33:00Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T03:00:06Z"}

{"id":"compress-claude-taf","title":"Optimize Huffman decoder with lookup tables","description":"The current Huffman decoder in flate/decompress decodes one bit at a time. Go uses a two-level table lookup (chunks + links) that decodes multiple bits per lookup. This is the #1 bottleneck — decompression is 49x slower than Go.

**Current**: bit-by-bit tree traversal in `HuffmanDecoder::decode`
**Target**: single-lookup for codes ≤9 bits, two-level for longer codes
**Impact**: Should bring decompression from ~49x to ~5-10x slower than Go","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Huffman decoder uses precomputed lookup table (≥9-bit first level)
- [ ] flate decompress benchmark improves by at least 5x
- [ ] All existing tests still pass","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T02:32:43Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T02:32:43Z"}

{"id":"compress-claude-taf","title":"Optimize Huffman decoder with lookup tables","description":"The current Huffman decoder in flate/decompress decodes one bit at a time. Go uses a two-level table lookup (chunks + links) that decodes multiple bits per lookup. This is the #1 bottleneck — decompression is 49x slower than Go.

**Current**: bit-by-bit tree traversal in `HuffmanDecoder::decode`
**Target**: single-lookup for codes ≤9 bits, two-level for longer codes
**Impact**: Should bring decompression from ~49x to ~5-10x slower than Go","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Huffman decoder uses precomputed lookup table (≥9-bit first level)
- [ ] flate decompress benchmark improves by at least 5x
- [ ] All existing tests still pass","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T02:32:43Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T03:00:47Z"}

{"id":"compress-claude-rlq","title":"Optimize DEFLATE compressor","description":"DEFLATE compression is 2-6x slower than Go depending on data type. Key areas:
- Hash chain lookup optimization
- Token encoding efficiency
- Lazy matching for levels 4-9
- Random data is 6x slower (worst case)","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] flate compress benchmarks improve by at least 2x
- [ ] All flate tests still pass","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T02:33:43Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T02:33:43Z"}

{"id":"compress-claude-y5b","title":"Expand benchmarks to larger data sizes and full corpus","description":"Per PLAN.md Section 9b, current benchmarks only cover 10KB data. Expand to:

- 100KB text workload
- 1MB text, zeros, random workloads
- JSON payload
- Binary payload
- bzip2 decompression benchmarks (currently missing)
- Per-level benchmarks for all compression levels

Also add tools/go_bench_test.go for automated Go comparison.","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Benchmarks cover the full corpus from PLAN.md Section 9b
- [ ] bzip2 decompression benchmarks added
- [ ] Go benchmark harness in tools/ directory","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T02:33:44Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T02:33:44Z"}

{"id":"compress-claude-a8g","title":"Eliminate Ref[T] indirection in inflate hot path","description":"The inflate decompression loop uses Ref[UInt64] and Ref[Int] for bit_buf, bit_count, and pos. Each access goes through heap-allocated Ref indirection. Convert to a struct with mutable fields passed directly, or restructure to avoid Ref entirely. Benchmarks show 12-57x slower than Go decompressor.","acceptance_criteria":"Ref[T] removed from decode_huffman_block hot path. Decompress benchmark improves measurably. All tests pass.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T10:31:22Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T10:31:22Z"}

{"id":"compress-claude-55v","title":"Optimize DictDecoder for bulk operations","description":"DictDecoder is the biggest decompression bottleneck (12-57x slower than Go). Current byte-at-a-time write_byte/write_copy and read_flush are extremely slow. Optimize with bulk memory copies, batch writes, and reduce per-byte overhead. This is the #1 decompression optimization target.","acceptance_criteria":"DEFLATE decompress 10KB benchmark improves by at least 2x. All existing tests pass.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T10:31:14Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T10:31:14Z"}

{"id":"compress-claude-rcx","title":"Optimize LZW decompressor throughput","description":"LZW decompression is 11-20x slower than Go. Main bottlenecks: byte-at-a-time output, dictionary table management, and bit reading overhead. Optimize with bulk string output, pre-allocated output buffer, and faster bit extraction.","acceptance_criteria":"LZW decompress 10KB benchmark improves by at least 2x. All tests pass.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T10:31:40Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T10:31:40Z"}

{"id":"compress-claude-6xa","title":"Dynamic Huffman block encoding for compressor","description":"Currently the compressor only emits fixed Huffman blocks. Dynamic Huffman blocks with custom code tables tuned to the actual symbol frequencies produce significantly smaller output, especially for larger inputs. Go's compressor uses dynamic blocks by default. This affects compression ratio more than speed.","acceptance_criteria":"Compressor emits dynamic Huffman blocks when they produce smaller output than fixed blocks. Compression ratio improves on text data. All tests pass.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T10:31:35Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T10:31:35Z"}

{"id":"compress-claude-2qx","title":"Level-differentiated DEFLATE compression strategies","description":"Currently all compression levels 1-9 use the same LZ77 algorithm with only chain length varying. Go uses completely different strategies per level (e.g. snappy-like for level 1, lazy matching for 4-6, optimal parsing for 7-9). Implement at least: (1) fast token-based encoder for level 1 without hash chains, (2) lazy matching for levels 4+. Compression is 1.3-4x slower than Go at 10KB.","acceptance_criteria":"BestSpeed (level 1) compress benchmark improves by at least 30%. Compression ratio at DefaultCompression stays within 5% of current. All tests pass.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T10:31:29Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T10:31:29Z"}

{"id":"compress-claude-2qx","title":"Level-differentiated DEFLATE compression strategies","description":"Currently all compression levels 1-9 use the same LZ77 algorithm with only chain length varying. Go uses completely different strategies per level (e.g. snappy-like for level 1, lazy matching for 4-6, optimal parsing for 7-9). Implement at least: (1) fast token-based encoder for level 1 without hash chains, (2) lazy matching for levels 4+. Compression is 1.3-4x slower than Go at 10KB.","acceptance_criteria":"BestSpeed (level 1) compress benchmark improves by at least 30%. Compression ratio at DefaultCompression stays within 5% of current. All tests pass.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T10:31:29Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T10:49:45Z"}

{"id":"compress-claude-ceg","title":"Golden file cross-validation with Go","description":"Per PLAN.md Section 8: create Go-generated golden files and cross-validation infrastructure.

1. tools/generate_golden.go — generates compressed files with Go stdlib
2. testdata/golden/ — golden compressed files + manifest.json
3. MoonBit tests that decompress Go-compressed data (T1)
4. Go tests that decompress MoonBit-compressed data (T3)
5. tools/cross_validate.go — cross-validation harness","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Golden file generator creates files for all algorithms × levels
- [ ] MoonBit can decompress all Go-generated golden files
- [ ] Go can decompress MoonBit-compressed output","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T02:33:44Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T02:33:44Z"}

{"id":"compress-claude-nqn","title":"Edge case and error handling test expansion","description":"Per PLAN.md test matrix (Section 8b), expand test coverage:

- T4: More error cases (truncated, corrupt headers, bad checksums)
- T5: Edge cases (1-byte input, 32KB boundary, all-0xFF, random data)
- T6: Async stream cancellation tests
- T7: Streaming chunked round-trip (compress in 256-byte chunks)

Current: 122 tests. Go has ~5,100 LOC of tests — significant gap.","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] At least 50 additional test cases across all algorithms
- [ ] Edge cases for each format boundary condition
- [ ] Error handling tests for each error variant","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T02:33:44Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T02:33:44Z"}

{"id":"compress-claude-nwp","title":"README and package documentation","description":"Create comprehensive documentation:

1. Top-level README.md with usage examples for all three layers
2. Per-package README.mbt.md with docstring tests
3. API documentation via moon ide doc
4. Performance comparison table (MoonBit vs Go)","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Top-level README with quick-start examples
- [ ] Each Layer 1 package has README.mbt.md with tested examples
- [ ] Benchmark results documented","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T02:33:44Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T02:33:44Z"}
